success_surface: Formula correction in Excel Table with structured references
effective_behaviors: Library selection matched task requirements (openpyxl for formula manipulation)
Pre-modification inspection to understand sheet structure and data types
Preserved existing structured reference format in formulas (Table2[[#This Row],...])
Logical condition ordering in IF statements follows priority hierarchy
Batch cell updates using row iteration across specified ranges
Output to new file path to preserve source integrity
Verification attempts to confirm changes were applied correctly
evidence: Used openpyxl.load_workbook() instead of pandas for formula cell-level manipulation
Inspected column headers and J6 value before crafting corrected formula
Recognized structured reference format 'Table2[[#This Row],[Column Name]]' from existing formula
Ordered conditions: empty check → prepaid/negative check → day-based conditions
Applied formula to J6:J10 using range(6, 11) iteration loop
Saved to output_path rather than overwriting source file
Printed verification messages for updated cells despite encountering .formula attribute limitation

memory_items.title: Formula Library Selection
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl for direct formula manipulation at the cell level; use pandas for data transformation and aggregation
memory_items.generalization: Match the tool to the operation type—cell-level formula editing requires workbook-level APIs rather than dataframe operations

memory_items.title: Structured Reference Preservation
memory_items.lesson: When updating formulas in Excel Tables, maintain the existing structured reference syntax (e.g., TableName[[#This Row],ColumnName])
memory_items.generalization: Inspect current formula patterns before rewriting to ensure compatibility with Excel Table features

memory_items.title: Conditional Logic Ordering
memory_items.lesson: Order nested IF conditions by specificity and business priority—handle special cases (negative values, blanks) before general thresholds
memory_items.generalization: Validate edge cases first in multi-condition formulas to prevent incorrect branching

memory_items.title: Batch Cell Update Pattern
memory_items.lesson: For multi-cell formula applications, iterate over row numbers within the target range using Python loops
memory_items.generalization: Apply consistent patterns for bulk operations: determine range bounds, iterate systematically, apply uniform transformations

memory_items.title: Formula Attribute Access Limitation
memory_items.lesson: In openpyxl, Cell objects may not expose .formula directly in all versions; use ._formula or alternative verification methods
memory_items.generalization: Test formula access patterns during inspection phase before relying on them for verification code

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons
- **Formula Library Selection**: Use openpyxl for direct formula manipulation at the cell level; use pandas for data transformation and aggregation.
- **Structured Reference Preservation**: When updating formulas in Excel Tables, maintain the existing structured reference syntax (e.g., TableName[[#This Row],ColumnName]) to ensure compatibility.
- **Conditional Logic Ordering**: Order nested IF conditions by specificity and business priority—handle edge cases (blanks, negative values) before general thresholds.
- **Batch Cell Update Pattern**: For multi-cell formula applications, iterate over row numbers within the target range using Python loops for consistency.
- **Formula Attribute Access Limitation**: In openpyxl, Cell objects may not expose .formula directly in all versions; test access patterns during inspection before verification.
